parentQuestion: A person has gone for a deep-tissue therapeutic massage. Which of the following receptors would be stimulated by the massage?

scaffolds.q: Deep-tissue massage involves applying strong pressure to muscles.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Unlike gentle Swedish massage, deep-tissue work uses significant pressure to reach deeper muscle layers. It can be uncomfortable or even painful.

scaffolds.q: The 'hurts so good' feeling during deep-tissue massage suggests pain receptors are involved.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! That intense, bordering-on-painful sensation comes from activating nociceptors (pain receptors). You're getting therapeutic pain.

scaffolds.q: Free nerve endings are the simplest type of sensory receptor - just bare nerve terminals.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Free nerve endings have no fancy capsules or structures. They're raw nerve terminals that respond to intense stimuli.

scaffolds.q: Free nerve endings detect painful, intense, or potentially damaging stimuli.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Free nerve endings (nociceptors) respond when stimuli are intense enough to be concerning - strong pressure, extreme temperature, or tissue damage.

scaffolds.q: Pacinian corpuscles detect vibration and brief pressure, not sustained deep pressure.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Pacinian corpuscles adapt rapidly - they fire when pressure starts and stops, but go quiet during sustained pressure. They're vibration detectors.

scaffolds.q: Golgi tendon organs detect muscle tension, not external pressure applied to skin.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Golgi tendon organs are in tendons and respond when YOUR muscle contracts forcefully. They don't detect someone pressing on you from outside.

scaffolds.q: The intense sensation of deep-tissue massage comes from stimulating free nerve endings (nociceptors).
scaffolds.explain: Yes! That uncomfortable-yet-therapeutic sensation is your nociceptors firing. You're essentially paying someone to activate your pain system in a controlled way.

scaffolds.q: Free nerve endings are polymodal - they respond to multiple types of intense stimuli.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Free nerve endings can respond to intense mechanical pressure, extreme temperature, AND damaging chemicals. They're the body's general alarm system.

scaffolds.q: The receptor most stimulated by deep-tissue massage is the ___.
scaffolds.explain: Free nerve endings (nociceptors)! The intense pressure of deep-tissue work is strong enough to activate these pain receptors. That's why it 'hurts so good.'
